competitor_patterns: Top rankers (Bikerumor, Geeky Gadgets) publish extremely long-form content: 4,000–19,000 words with multiple products tested head-to-head, giving Google extensive topical coverage — our longest post is 3,474 words

Bikerumor uses individual H2s per product reviewed (e.g., 'Julbo Density', 'Oakley Sutro Lite') creating structured, crawlable product comparison sections with first-hand test data and dates (May 21, 2026)
Top competitors use explicit 'Tested' or 'Reviewed' signals in meta titles and H1s, building E-E-A-T credibility — Bikerumor: 'Bikerumor Tested', Geeky Gadgets: 'How the Models Were Evaluated'
Geeky Gadgets structures content around price tiers ('$100 to $1,500') making it useful for budget-conscious searchers at every stage of the funnel — a pattern absent from our posts
Competitor snippets consistently include a specific date (May 21, 2026) in the meta description, which increases CTR by signalling recency to searchers who include '2026' in their query
our_gaps: Word count is critically short on most posts (1,104–1,259 words) vs. competitors averaging 4,000–19,000 words — Google rewards topical depth; our photochromic, half-frame, and Sigma posts are far too thin to rank competitively

We lack independent, structured product comparison tables that pit StradaPro against 4–6 named rivals on spec-by-spec metrics (weight, lens system, UV rating, price, trial policy) — Bikerumor does this per product with H2s

No explicit test/ride methodology section — competitors signal E-E-A-T with 'how we tested' sections; our posts rely on brand narrative without third-party or first-person ride-test credibility markers

Our meta titles for 'veluto' (misspelling variant with 298 impressions, 0.3% CTR) are almost certainly not optimised for that spelling variant — we are losing ~297 impressions/month for free with a simple fix

We have zero content targeting 'interchangeable lens cycling glasses' despite that being a core StradaPro differentiator and a keyword competitor Allsportssunglassesusa ranks in top 3 for — a direct product-feature gap
keyword_opportunities: interchangeable lens cycling glasses — competitor Allsportssunglassesusa ranks top 3; directly matches StradaPro's VellutoPuro + VellutoVisione click-in lens system — zero coverage from us

lightweight cycling sunglasses 25g — no competitor owns this ultra-specific weight niche; StradaPro's 25g frame is a concrete, searchable differentiator that competes with 'ultralight cycling glasses'
anti-fog cycling glasses road bike — anti-fog is a StradaPro feature and a high-intent purchase query with low brand competition in organic results
cycling sunglasses 30-day trial — nearly zero competition for this trial-policy angle; captures bottom-of-funnel buyers who are risk-averse and comparing purchase confidence
tifosi rail alternative 2026 — Bikerumor ranks Tifosi Rail prominently; searchers looking for alternatives after reading that review are a warm audience we can intercept with a direct comparison post
next_post_guidelines: Target minimum 3,500 words for any 'best cycling glasses' or comparison post — use one H2 per product/topic section, mirror Bikerumor's structure of named product H2s to capture long-tail product-name queries

Open every post published in July 2026 with an explicit publish/update date in the first paragraph AND in the meta description (e.g., 'Updated July 2026 — tested on real rides') to compete for '2026' year-modifier queries where recency signals CTR

Add a 'How We Tested' H2 section (150–200 words) describing real-ride conditions (road surface, weather, distance) — this is the single biggest E-E-A-T gap vs. Bikerumor and directly impacts ranking for 'review' and 'tested' queries
Include a price-tier comparison table for any 'best cycling sunglasses' post — segment by budget: under €80, €80–€150, €150+ — with StradaPro anchored in the mid-tier as the value-for-spec winner

For the Oakley alternatives post (positions 6.8–7.0, 320 combined impressions), expand the existing post to 5,000+ words by adding individual H2 sections for each Oakley alternative (Tifosi, POC, Julbo, Knockaround, StradaPro) with spec tables per product — this mirrors the exact structure that earns Bikerumor's position 1

Embed a structured FAQ schema block of at least 5 Q&As per post targeting question-format queries ('Are Oakley sunglasses good for cycling?', 'What are the lightest cycling glasses?') — increases eligibility for Google AI Overview and People Also Ask placements

For all posts targeting 'best cycling glasses 2026' and 'oakley alternatives', add an explicit Velluto StradaPro spec card (weight: 25g, lenses: 2 included, trial: 30 days, UV: UV400, anti-fog: yes) as a styled HTML table near the top — give Google a clear structured data anchor for product feature queries
seo_quick_wins: Add publish date + 'Updated [Month Year]' to every meta description — our near-top keywords ('best cycling glasses 2026', position 7.3; 'best cycling sunglasses 2026', position 8.8) lose CTR to competitors whose snippets show explicit 2026 dates; this is a zero-development fix in the CMS

Add HowTo or ItemList schema markup to all comparison/roundup posts — Bikerumor and Geeky Gadgets dominate rich results partly through structured data; adding schema to our 'best Oakley alternative' post (already has table + FAQ) can unlock featured snippet eligibility from current position 7

Create a canonical 'veluto' redirect or add 'veluto' as a secondary keyword target in the meta of the Velluto homepage/brand page — 298 impressions at 0.3% CTR means ~297 users per month who misspell the brand name are not clicking; a title tag that includes the misspelling variant or an explicit disambiguation line ('Also spelled: Veluto') recovers this traffic at zero content cost
meta_title_fixes: QUERY: veluto → New title: Velluto Cycling Glasses — Official Store (veluto) | Why: Captures the misspelling variant organically in parentheses, confirms brand identity to confused searchers, lifts CTR from 0.3% by removing ambiguity about whether the result is the right brand

QUERY: best cycling sunglasses 2026 → New title: Best Cycling Sunglasses 2026 — Tested & Ranked | Why: Adds 'Tested & Ranked' to match Bikerumor's credibility signal that dominates this SERP; stays under 60 chars and front-loads the exact query match

QUERY: oakley alternatives → New title: Best Oakley Alternatives 2026 | Cycling Glasses | Why: Front-loads the query, adds '2026' recency signal, and adds 'Cycling' to qualify intent — increases relevance match score vs. current generic title

QUERY: oakley sunglasses alternative → New title: Oakley Sunglasses Alternatives — Lighter & Cheaper | Why: Uses comparative language ('Lighter & Cheaper') that answers the implicit intent (find something better/different) and differentiates from Oakley's own listings dominating the SERP

QUERY: best cycling sunglasses → New title: Best Cycling Sunglasses 2026 — 25g, Anti-Fog, UV400 | Why: Injects concrete spec numbers (25g, anti-fog, UV400) that act as visual differentiators in the SERP snippet and speak directly to purchase-intent searchers comparing specs
content_quick_wins: QUERY: velluto (pos 5.0, 1139 impressions) → Topic: Velluto StradaPro — Complete Brand & Product Guide 2026 | Add: Expand the homepage or create a dedicated '/velluto-stradapro' landing page with full spec table, 30-day trial explainer, lens comparison (VellutoPuro vs VellutoVisione), size guide, and 10+ customer ride reviews to push branded position from 5 to top 3 and raise CTR above current 5.6%

QUERY: best cycling sunglasses 2026 (pos 8.8, 218 impressions) → Topic: Best Cycling Sunglasses 2026 — 18 Models Tested by Real Riders | Add: Long-form roundup (5,000+ words) with individual H2 per model, a ride-test methodology section, price-tier table, and StradaPro as the 'best value' winner — mirrors Bikerumor's structure that holds position 1

QUERY: oakley alternatives (pos 7.0, 202 impressions) + oakley alternative (pos 6.8) + oakley sunglasses alternative (pos 6.9) → Topic: Best Oakley Alternatives for Cycling 2026 — Expand existing post to 5,000 words | Add: Individual H2 sections for Tifosi, POC, Knockaround, Julbo, and Velluto StradaPro each with a spec comparison table, add 'Why riders switch from Oakley' section, embed 5-star review quotes, add ItemList schema

QUERY: best cycling glasses 2026 (pos 7.3, 141 impressions) → Topic: Best Cycling Glasses 2026 — Road, Gravel & MTB Ranked | Add: Separate the existing content into use-case sections (road, gravel, commuting, budget), add a comparison table with columns for weight/lens system/UV/price/trial policy, include StradaPro in 'best road cycling glasses' and 'best value' categories — target 4,500 words minimum

QUERY: best cycling glasses (pos 8.8, 45 impressions) → Topic: Best Cycling Glasses — The Complete 2026 Buyer's Guide | Add: Create an evergreen buyer's guide page (separate from the '2026' post) targeting the non-year-modified query, covering lens tint guide, frame fit types, anti-fog explainer, and UV certification — this captures year-round search volume and builds internal link authority to the '2026' roundup
geo_angles: Position StradaPro as the answer to 'What is the lightest cycling sunglasses with interchangeable lenses?' — AI engines like Perplexity and ChatGPT pull factual, spec-specific answers; publishing a page that explicitly states '25g frame, two included lenses (clear + high-contrast), tool-free click-in system, UV400, 30-day trial' in a structured FAQ gives AI crawlers a clean, citable fact block to surface in generated answers

Create a 'Velluto StradaPro vs [Top 5 Competitors]' summary table page optimised for the query 'best cycling sunglasses comparison 2026' — AI Overviews and ChatGPT responses frequently cite comparison tables from brand-neutral or brand-owned pages when they contain structured, verifiable data (weight, price, lens count, trial policy) not buried in prose

Publish a dedicated '30-Day Cycling Sunglasses Trial — How It Works' page and reference it in every post — AI assistants answering 'can I try cycling sunglasses before buying' or 'which cycling glasses have a free trial' will cite the most explicit, standalone explanation of the policy; this also targets a zero-competition niche query that converts at the bottom of the funnel
comparison_post_angles: COMPETITOR: Bikerumor — angle: 'We rode the same 18 models Bikerumor tested — here's what they missed about the StradaPro' — headline idea: 'Bikerumor's Best Cycling Sunglasses 2026 List — And the 25g Italian Frame They Overlooked' | Key differentiator: Bikerumor's top picks (Julbo, Tifosi, POC) all weigh more and lack a 30-day trial; Velluto advantage to lead with: 25g ultralight frame + risk-free 30-day trial not offered by any Bikerumor-ranked product

COMPETITOR: Geeky Gadgets — angle: 'Smart glasses vs. traditional cycling glasses — why 25g beats connectivity for most road cyclists in 2026' — headline idea: 'Best Cycling Glasses 2026: Do You Actually Need Smart Lenses or Just Better Optics?' | Key differentiator: Geeky Gadgets ranks for 'best cycling glasses 2026' with smart/navigation glasses content that doesn't serve traditional road cyclists; Velluto advantage to lead with: VellutoVisione high-contrast lens system outperforms smart glass optics for performance riding at a fraction of the price

COMPETITOR: Allsportssunglassesusa — angle: 'Interchangeable lens cycling glasses — why the click-in system matters more than the brand name' — headline idea: 'Best Interchangeable Lens Cycling Glasses 2026: Tool-Free vs. Screw-In Systems Compared' | Key differentiator: Allsportssunglassesusa ranks top 3 for 'interchangeable lens cycling glasses' with a broad catalogue approach; Velluto advantage to lead with: StradaPro's tool-free click-in lens swap (VellutoPuro + VellutoVisione included in the box) vs. competitors who charge extra for second lenses
